MBZUAI is seeking an Executive Director to provide overall vision, leadership, and strategic direction for the Institute for Agriculture & AI (IAAI). The Executive Director is responsible for shaping and executing the Institute’s mission at the intersection of artificial intelligence, agriculture, and sustainability. The role oversees strategy development, institutional management, partnership building, and resource mobilization, while representing the Institute to funders, government entities, industry partners, and the global research community. The Executive Director ensures the Institute’s long-term impact, relevance, and financial sustainability. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership and Vision Define and lead the Institute’s long-term vision, strategy, and priorities at the intersection of artificial intelligence, agriculture, and sustainability, in alignment with MBZUAI’s mission and global agendas. Translate complex agricultural and food-system challenges into AI-driven research, innovation programs, and scalable solutions with measurable impact. Position the Institute as a globally recognized center of excellence for responsible and impactful AI applications in agriculture. Establish and oversee planning, budgeting, and reporting frameworks to ensure financial sustainability and institutional resilience. Ensure alignment with policies, governance structures, and strategic priorities. Partnerships, Fundraising, and External Engagement Act as the primary representative of the Institute to governments, donors, industry partners, and international organizations. Lead the engineering function with a hands-on approach, including conducting architecture and code reviews, establishing technical standards, and making key build vs. buy decisions. Own and evolve the end-to-end technical architecture of the company’s products and platforms, including system design, technology stack selection, data architecture, and engineering best practices. Design, implement, and oversee robust security architecture across all products, including threat modeling, secure SDLC practices, penetration testing, and incident response planning. Stay abreast of emerging technologies and industry trends, leading research and development initiatives to evaluate and adopt new solutions that support business growth. Evaluate, select, and manage technology vendors and partners, including contract negotiation and performance management to ensure optimal value delivery. Oversee the technology budget, ensuring effective allocation of resources across strategic and operational initiatives while identifying cost optimization opportunities. Collaborate closely with executive leadership and cross-functional teams to align technology initiatives with business priorities, ensuring clear communication and stakeholder engagement. Ensure compliance with relevant laws, regulations, and industry standards related to technology, data privacy, and information security, implementing policies to mitigate risk and maintain governance. Requirements Demonstrated hands-on experience in modern software development within the past 3–5 years. Strong ability to conduct code reviews and assess software architecture, ensuring scalability, performance, and maintainability. Proven experience working with cloud-native architectures (AWS, Azure, or GCP), along with containerization technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines. Experience designing and developing APIs, microservices, and distributed systems in complex environments. Solid understanding of modern security engineering practices, extending beyond governance into implementation. Hands-on proficiency in at least one modern programming language, such as Python, Go, Java, or TypeScript. Candidates must have recent, practical experience in software development or system architecture within the last 5 years. Profiles lacking this will not be considered. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field; a Master’s degree is preferred. 20+ years of experience in senior technology leadership roles, with substantial hands-on expertise in software development and system architecture. Proven track record of driving technology innovation, including the design, development, and successful implementation of scalable solutions. Strong leadership and communication skills, with the ability to inspire, mentor, and manage high-performing engineering teams. Demonstrated experience in shaping innovation strategy, as well as managing technology budgets, resource planning, and vendor relationships. The role will expand the structured credit mandate, developing scalable exposure to private ABS, warehouse facilities, forward-flow agreements, and securitization take-outs, primarily targeting senior and super-senior risk (AAA/AA/A) within the Bank’s risk appetite. Working in close partnership with the Head of Investments and Head of Fixed Income Investments, the Portfolio Manager will originate, structure, and manage proprietary investment frameworks, contributing to diversification, recurring income generation, and long-term growth of structured credit portfolios. Key Accountabilities Construct and actively manage portfolios of private ABS, with exposure to SME lending, consumer finance, fintech-originated receivables, and specialty asset classes. Originate and execute forward-flow transactions with fintech platforms, non-bank lenders, and specialty finance companies. Develop and oversee warehouse, prefunding, and term securitization structures, including transition from private ABS to rated public take-outs where relevant. Design robust investment frameworks for private ABS, ensuring compliance with internal risk appetite, regulatory constraints, and capital treatment. Maintain active dialogue with fintech platforms, originators, servicers, arrangers, and rating agencies to source opportunities and manage asset performance. Implement strong risk monitoring, performance analytics, and P&L attribution frameworks for private and illiquid structured credit portfolios. Integrate macroeconomic, relative value, and structural analysis into investment decision-making. Apply advanced modelling and structuring expertise, including cash-flow modelling, stress testing, and scenario analysis for private ABS. Identify new asset classes (e.g. BNPL, merchant cash advance, trade receivables, digital SME lending) consistent with the Bank’s risk appetite Specific Accountability As this mandate will lead to new portfolios and asset classes, the Portfolio Manager will: Build the end-to-end investment and risk infrastructure (credit, legal, operational, servicing). Establish private ABS and forward-flow governance frameworks within the Bank. Act as a center of expertise on private structured credit, supporting Risk, Legal, Compliance, Treasury, and Finance teams. Ensure scalability from bilateral / private transactions to securitized formats. Maintain strong market visibility and represent the Bank across global private ABS and fintech credit ecosystems. Collaborate with internal teams on: Capital efficiency and regulatory treatment, Liquidity management, Accounting and valuation of private assets Qualifications & Experience Minimum
